key_insight: The paper extends decentralized online Riemannian optimization to strongly geodesically convex losses and achieves the first O(log T) static regret guarantees, including in a two-point bandit setting.
engineering_relevance: This provides a provably efficient learning framework for distributed systems operating on curved parameter spaces, such as manifold-valued sensing, robotics, and synchronization problems.
method: Derive a general network-error analysis that works with time-varying step-size schedules.
Use decentralized online Riemannian gradient descent with decaying step sizes to match the strongly g-convex regime.
Extend the analysis to two-point bandit feedback via strong subconvexity of smoothed losses.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical regret bounds of O(log T) for both full-information and two-point bandit decentralized online Riemannian optimization.
watch_item: The guarantees depend on bounded sectional curvature and carefully chosen decaying step sizes, so performance may be sensitive to geometry assumptions and network topology in practice.
paper_title: Decentralized Online Riemannian Optimization for Strongly Geodesically Convex Functions
